files.description: Execution standards: model dispatch, nix requirements, code density bar, shell and provisioning law, commit grammar
Repo role and ownership boundaries for agents
Repo structure register: module split, overlays, services, hosts
Universal design law binding every executable surface: rails, dispatch, admitted substrate, parameterization, boundaries, density, vocabularies
Nix module graph law: owner modules, row dispatch, overlays, package manifest, flake roots, both-OS eval
Shell kernel law: bash/jq division, catalog dispatch, JSON envelope rails, receipts, locks, fork discipline
Consumer law: Lua projection consumers and generated declarative config, vocabulary owners
Service-agent law: launchd identity and lifecycle, remote transport, dual-OS rows
Schema-v3 JSON envelope contract for provisioning commands
TypeScript doctrine: Effect rails, services, boundaries
Python doctrine: typed rails, uv custody
The trap ledger: each paid-for failure and the rule that now forecloses it, anchored to its owning surface
Seam map: config.forge.* option hinges, runtime contracts (receipts, terminal mesh, XDG projections), owner tables — the blast radius a cross-module change must honor
Estate rails and contract surfaces: forge-* command rails, receipts, provision envelopes, registers
Prose law: sentence altitude, register, banned patterns
Information architecture law: structure, density, tables vs prose
Edit-coupling map: which surfaces obligate which counterparts in the same change
Admission law for the docs/laws corpus: what earns a law page and how findings land
